topic: Claude Opus 4.7 vs 4.6 vs Mythos Comparison 2026: Which Model Should You Use?

key_points: Side-by-side three-model comparison covering all three Anthropic tiers
Focus on which model fits which use case
Covers availability restrictions on Mythos Preview
Discusses tokenizer cost implications of Opus 4.7
Focuses on what actually changed between 4.7 and 4.6
CursorBench: 58% (4.6) vs 70% (4.7) — 12-point jump
Vision improvements: 3.3x more pixel area per image
More literal instruction following in 4.7 vs loose in 4.6
Tokenizer change: up to 35% more tokens for same text
SWE-bench Verified: 80.8% (4.6) → 87.6% (4.7), +6.8 pts
Same pricing $5/$25 per million input/output tokens
Production task completion rate 3x higher on 4.7
Mythos released April 7, 2026 as gated research preview
SWE-bench Verified: 93.9% (+13.1 pts over Opus 4.6)
USAMO 2026: 97.6% (+55.3 pts over Opus 4.6)
Only available via Project Glasswing to 12 major tech/finance companies
Pricing $25/$125 per million input/output tokens
Agentic coding comparison focus
Task budgets: new feature in 4.7 for agentic token control
thinking.budget_tokens removed — must migrate to effort levels
Opus 4.7 excels on long-horizon autonomous coding tasks
Partner saw 13% higher resolution on 93-task coding benchmark

stats.fact: Claude Opus 4.7 scores 70% on CursorBench vs 58% for Opus 4.6 — a 12-point improvement on real-world IDE coding tasks
SWE-bench Verified: Opus 4.7 87.6% vs Opus 4.6 80.8% (+6.8 pts); SWE-bench Pro: 64.3% vs 53.4% (+10.9 pts)
Claude Mythos Preview achieves 93.9% on SWE-bench Verified — 13.1 percentage points above Opus 4.6 and the highest score ever recorded
Claude Mythos USAMO 2026 score: 97.6% — a 55.3-point leap over Opus 4.6 (42.3%) and 2.4 pts above GPT-5.4 (95.2%)
Opus 4.7 tokenizer uses up to 35% more tokens than Opus 4.6 for the same text (especially code, JSON, non-English)
Opus 4.7 pricing unchanged at $5/$25 per million input/output tokens; Mythos Preview priced at $25/$125
Opus 4.7 image resolution cap raised to 2,576px long edge (~3.75 MP) vs ~1,568px (~1.15 MP) on 4.6 — 3.3x more pixel area
Partner testing showed Opus 4.7 solves 3x more production tasks than Opus 4.6
Mythos autonomously discovered thousands of zero-day vulnerabilities across every major OS and browser, including an OpenBSD bug that had been present for 27 years
Opus 4.7 MCP-Atlas (agentic tool use) score: 77.3%, significantly ahead of GPT-5.4 and Gemini 3.1 Pro among publicly available models
Opus 4.7 BrowseComp regression: 79.3% vs 83.7% for Opus 4.6 — web research agents relying on multi-page synthesis should test carefully before upgrading
Anthropic committed $100M in usage credits to Project Glasswing; 12 companies including Amazon, Apple, Google, Microsoft, Nvidia participating
Mythos succeeds 73% of the time on expert-level cybersecurity tasks that no model could complete before April 2025
Claude Opus 4.7 released April 16, 2026; Mythos Preview released April 7, 2026

angles: Three-tier breakdown: which model is publicly available vs gated vs obsolete — most readers assume all three are equal options
The tokenizer trap: Opus 4.7's 'same price' headline hides a real cost increase of up to 35% for code/JSON workloads
Prompt compatibility risk: Opus 4.7's stricter literal instruction-following can break prompts tuned for 4.6's looser behavior
Mythos is not a product choice — it's a policy decision: only 12 companies in the world can access it, and it's exclusively for defensive cybersecurity
The 4.6 retention case: for stable production systems with tuned prompts, staying on 4.6 is a legitimate, defensible choice — not tech debt
Agentic coding is where 4.7 earns its keep: 3x production task resolution rate and new task budget controls for long-horizon agents
Vision upgrade inflection: 3.3x pixel area improvement makes 4.7 the first Opus model usable for dense chart/document processing
Safety-capability trade-off on display: Anthropic built Mythos but won't ship it broadly — a case study in responsible scaling policy
Migration checklist angle: exact steps developers need to migrate from 4.6 to 4.7 (thinking API changes, tokenizer testing, prompt audit)
keywords: should i upgrade claude opus 4.6 to 4.7
